 {"id":"beads_rust-hee","title":"quick Command Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br quick` command for rapid issue lookup by ID prefix with minimal output. Designed for scripting and quick checks.

## CLI Interface
```
br quick <partial-id> [OPTIONS]

Arguments:
  <partial-id>              Partial issue ID to resolve

Options:
  --field <FIELD>           Output specific field only (id, title, status, priority, type)
  --json                    Output as JSON
```

## Technical Requirements

### Implementation
```rust
fn cmd_quick(partial: &str, field: Option<&str>) -> Result<()> {
    let storage = open_storage()?;
    let full_id = storage.resolve_partial_id(partial)?;
    let issue = storage.get_issue(&full_id)?
        .ok_or_else(|| BeadsError::IssueNotFound(partial.into()))?;
    
    match field {
        Some("id") => println!("{}", issue.id),
        Some("title") => println!("{}", issue.title),
        Some("status") => println!("{}", issue.status),
        Some("priority") => println!("{}", issue.priority),
        Some("type") => println!("{}", issue.issue_type),
        None => println!("{} {} {}", issue.id, issue.status, issue.title),
        Some(f) => return Err(BeadsError::UnknownField(f.into())),
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

### Use Cases
```bash
# Get full ID from prefix
br quick abc
# Output: bd-abc12

# Get just the title
br quick abc --field title
# Output: Fix authentication bug

# Use in scripts
STATUS=$(br quick abc --field status)
if [ "$STATUS" = "closed" ]; then
    echo "Already done"
fi
```

## Output Formats

### Default (single line)
```
bd-abc12 open Fix authentication bug
```

### --field (single value)
```
open
```

### --json
```json
{
  "id": "bd-abc12",
  "title": "Fix authentication bug",
  "status": "open",
  "priority": 1,
  "issue_type": "bug"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Resolve partial ID to full ID
- [ ] Output single line by default
- [ ] --field outputs just that field
- [ ] --json outputs full issue as JSON
- [ ] Error if ambiguous prefix
- [ ] Error if no match

## Unit Tests
- Exact ID match works
- Prefix match works
- Ambiguous prefix returns error
- No match returns error
- Each field flag works
- JSON output correct

## Dependencies
- ID Resolution & Prefix Matching
- SQLite Storage Layer Core

## Rationale
The quick command enables scripting and fast lookups. Integrations & Advanced:
Working With Issues:
  close              Close one or more issues
  comments           View or manage comments on an issue
  create             Create a new issue (or multiple issues from markdown file)
  create-form        Create a new issue using an interactive form
  delete             Delete one or more issues and clean up references
  edit               Edit an issue field in $EDITOR
  gate               Manage async coordination gates
  label              Manage issue labels
  list               List issues
  merge-slot         Manage merge-slot gates for serialized conflict resolution
  move               Move an issue to a different rig with dependency remapping
  q                  Quick capture: create issue and output only ID

  reopen             Reopen one or more closed issues
  search             Search issues by text query
  set-state          Set operational state (creates event + updates label)
  show               Show issue details
  state              Query the current value of a state dimension
  update             Update one or more issues

Views & Reports:
  activity           Show real-time molecule state feed
  count              Count issues matching filters
  lint               Check issues for missing template sections
  stale              Show stale issues (not updated recently)
  status             Show issue database overview and statistics

Dependencies & Structure:
  dep                Manage dependencies
  duplicate          Mark an issue as a duplicate of another
  duplicates         Find and optionally merge duplicate issues
  epic               Epic management commands
  graph              Display issue dependency graph
  supersede          Mark an issue as superseded by a newer one
  swarm              Swarm management for structured epics

Sync & Data:
  daemon             Manage background sync daemon
  export             Export issues to JSONL or Obsidian format
  import             Import issues from JSONL format
  merge              Git merge driver for beads JSONL files
  restore            Restore full history of a compacted issue from git
  sync               Synchronize issues with git remote

Setup & Configuration:
  config             Manage configuration settings
  hooks              Manage git hooks for bd auto-sync
  human              Show essential commands for human users

  worktree           Manage git worktrees for parallel development

Integrations & Advanced:

  linear             Linear integration commands
  repo               Manage multiple repository configuration

## Purpose
Display full issue details (including labels, dependencies, dependents, comments) with classic output format.

## CLI
```
br show <id...> [OPTIONS]
br show [OPTIONS]  # Uses last-touched if no ID
```

## Flags
- `<id...>`: One or more issue IDs (partial resolution supported).
- `--short`: Compact single-line output.
- `--deps`: Include dependency details.
- `--comments`: Include comments.
- `--events`: Include event history.
- `--refs`: Include external references.
- `--json`: JSON output.
- `--robot`: Machine-readable output (alias for --json).

## Behavior
1. Resolve ID(s) via partial matching.
2. Set last-touched to the first shown ID.
3. Fetch full issue details including:
   - All issue fields.
   - Labels (always included).
   - Dependencies (blocking deps).
   - Dependents (issues this blocks).
   - Comments (if `--comments` or full view).
   - Events (if `--events`).
   - Parent issue (if exists).
4. JSON output is ALWAYS an array (even for single ID).

## Output

### JSON (IssueDetails schema)
```json
[
  {
    "id": "bd-abc12",
    "title": "Implement feature X",
    "description": "Full description here...",
    "status": "open",
    "priority": 1,
    "issue_type": "feature",
    "assignee": "alice",
    "created_at": "2025-01-10T09:00:00Z",
    "labels": ["backend", "api"],
    "dependencies": [
      {"depends_on_id": "bd-xyz89", "type": "blocks"}
    ],
    "dependents": [
      {"issue_id": "bd-def34", "type": "blocks"}
    ],
    "comments": [
      {"id": 1, "author": "bob", "text": "LGTM", "created_at": "2025-01-11T10:00:00Z"}
    ],
    "parent": "bd-epic1"
  }
]
```

### Text Output (Full View)
```
bd-abc12: Implement feature X
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

```


## Data Structures

### IssueDetails
```rust
pub struct IssueDetails {
    #[serde(flatten)]
    pub issue: Issue,
    pub labels: Vec<String>,
    pub dependencies: Vec<DependencyInfo>,
    pub dependents: Vec<DependencyInfo>,
    pub comments: Vec<Comment>,
    pub parent: Option<String>,
}

pub struct DependencyInfo {
    pub issue_id: String,
    pub depends_on_id: String,
    pub dep_type: String,
    pub title: Option<String>,  // For display
    pub status: Option<String>, // For display
}
```

## Error Handling
- **IssueNotFound**: ID does not resolve → error with suggestions.
- **AmbiguousId**: ID resolves to multiple → error with candidate list.

## Logging
```rust
tracing::info!(ids = ?ids, "Showing issue details");
tracing::debug!(id = %id, "Resolved issue");
tracing::debug!(labels = ?labels, deps = ?deps.len(), "Fetched relations");
tracing::trace!(comments = ?comments.len(), "Loaded comments");
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- JSON array output matches bd.
- Text output matches golden snapshots.
- IssueDetails includes all relation types.
- Last-touched set correctly.

```rust
#[test]
fn test_show_basic() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Show test issue");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &id])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("Show test issue"))
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains(&id));
}

        .assert()
        .success();
    
    let id = get_last_issue_id(&beads_dir);
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &id])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("This is the full body"));

#[test]
fn test_show_with_dependencies() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    let blocker = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Blocking issue");
    let blocked = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Dependent issue");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["dep", "add", &blocked, &blocker])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Show blocked issue should list dependency
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &blocked])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains(&blocker).or(predicate::str::contains("Blocking issue")));
}

fn test_show_with_dependents() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    let blocker = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Blocking issue");
    let blocked = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Dependent issue");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["dep", "add", &blocked, &blocker])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Show blocker should list what it blocks

}
```
